env_vars_used.name: MINIO_USE_SSL
env_vars_used.note: Referenced in .env.production.example but never read by code

missing_from_env_example.name: GOOGLE_VEO_API_KEY
missing_from_env_example.note: Optional video provider API key, falls back to empty string

missing_from_env_example.name: OPENAI_API_KEY
missing_from_env_example.note: Optional video provider API key, falls back to empty string

missing_from_env_example.name: PORT
missing_from_env_example.note: Generic PORT used by all 3 Fastify services, set via ecosystem.config.js

hardcoded_secrets.description: Hardcoded JWT fallback secret used when JWT_SECRET env var is not set. Production guard exists (line 10-12) that throws if NODE_ENV=production, but in dev/test this weak secret is used for signing tokens.
Hardcoded MinIO access key fallback. Acceptable for local dev but would be a security issue if deployed without .env.
Hardcoded MinIO secret key fallback. Same value in shared config.ts default. Acceptable for local dev only.
Default database URL with dev credentials in Zod schema. Contains username 'airevstream' and password 'airevstream_dev'.
Hardcoded DATABASE_URL fallback with dev credentials in maintenance worker (used for pg_dump).
Hardcoded Redis URL fallback. No credentials exposed but hardcodes localhost assumption.
Default admin password in seed file. Expected for dev seeding but should be documented as requiring change.
Test password matching seed data. Acceptable for E2E test fixtures.

config_issues.description: Uses OLLAMA_URL but code reads OLLAMA_BASE_URL. Production deployments using this example will have a broken Ollama connection since the env var name does not match.

Missing several env vars that code references: NEXT_PUBLIC_APP_URL, CORS_ORIGINS, JWT_REFRESH_SECRET, REGISTRATION_DISABLED, COMFYUI_TIMEOUT_MS, TTS_BASE_URL, TTS_API_KEY. Production deployments may have issues if these are not set.

Content-Security-Policy includes 'unsafe-inline' and 'unsafe-eval' for script-src and 'unsafe-inline' for style-src. While common for Next.js apps, this weakens XSS protections. Consider using nonce-based CSP for production.

Several env vars read directly via process.env are not validated in the central Zod envSchema: PORT, GOOGLE_VEO_API_KEY, OPENAI_API_KEY, E2E_BASE_URL, TTS_API_KEY, TTS_BASE_URL, COMFYUI_TIMEOUT_MS, INSTAGRAM_API_VERSION, FACEBOOK_API_VERSION. These bypass centralized validation.
MINIO_USE_SSL is defined in .env.production.example but the storage package hardcodes useSSL: false (line 21) and never reads this env var. SSL will never be enabled via env config.
Storage package reads MINIO_* env vars directly (process.env.MINIO_*) instead of using the centralized getConfig() from @airevstream/shared. Same defaults are duplicated in both places.
Queue package reads REDIS_URL directly via process.env instead of using getConfig(). Default value is duplicated.
Ollama provider reads OLLAMA_BASE_URL directly via process.env in 5 places instead of using getConfig(). Default 'http://localhost:11434' is duplicated across all occurrences.
Production worker reads COMFYUI_URL and COMFYUI_TIMEOUT_MS directly via process.env instead of using getConfig().

ENCRYPTION_KEY and JWT_SECRET are optional in the Zod schema. The production guard only exists in api-server.ts for JWT_SECRET. No centralized guard prevents starting without ENCRYPTION_KEY in production, which would cause runtime errors when encrypting credentials.
globalDependencies includes .env which means any .env change invalidates all Turbo caches. This is correct behavior but may cause unnecessary rebuilds during local development.

Worker scripts reference dist/workers/*.js but workers build to workers/dist/ (the cwd is '.', not './workers'). Verify the built output path matches. If workers use a root-level build step, this is fine; otherwise the paths may be wrong.
Uses experimental.serverComponentsExternalPackages which was moved to a stable config key in Next.js 14.1+. Should use serverExternalPackages instead.
JWT_REFRESH_SECRET is defined in .env.example and the Zod config schema but is never referenced in any source code. It appears to be an unused env var.

missing_patterns.note: The .audit/ directory contains generated audit artifacts. Consider adding to .gitignore if these should not be committed.

critical_issues: HIGH: .env.production.example uses OLLAMA_URL but code reads OLLAMA_BASE_URL — production Ollama will be broken
HIGH: JWT fallback 'dev-secret-change-me' used when JWT_SECRET not set (production guard exists but dev/test insecure)
MEDIUM: MINIO_USE_SSL in .env.production.example is never read by code — SSL cannot be enabled via env config
MEDIUM: Several env vars bypass centralized Zod validation (read directly via process.env)
MEDIUM: No production guard for ENCRYPTION_KEY — will cause runtime errors encrypting credentials
MEDIUM: .env.production.example missing JWT_REFRESH_SECRET, NEXT_PUBLIC_APP_URL, CORS_ORIGINS, REGISTRATION_DISABLED
summary.gitignore_status: Good — all sensitive patterns properly ignored. .env files never committed to git history.
